Final Assessment
🔗 Multi-Chain Obfuscation Confirmed
🌉 Cross-Chain Bridge Services Used
🍌 Peeling Chain Behavior Detected
🚫 No Exit Point Identified Yet
🔍 Analyst Summary

The traced funds are currently distributed across four primary Bitcoin addresses, each holding exactly 0.16777215 BTC, with one residual output of $16.15. No outgoing transactions have been observed from any of these addresses, indicating that the funds remain under the control of the same threat actor. The attacker has not yet moved funds to an identifiable exchange or cashing service. Continued monitoring of all five addresses is recommended. The residual dust address in particular may provide the first indication of the attacker's next move.

Methodology & Tools Used
Methodology

This investigation was conducted using transaction-level on-chain analysis, tracing the movement of funds step-by-step across three blockchain networks — BNB Chain, TRON, and Bitcoin. Each transaction was individually verified through its Transaction ID (TXID) on the corresponding blockchain explorer, and wallet addresses were tracked through their full interaction history.

Cross-chain transfers were identified and traced using bridge-specific explorers (Bridgers.xyz and NEAR Intents), enabling continuity of the fund trail across networks that would otherwise appear disconnected. Fund consolidation and splitting behaviors — including peeling chain patterns — were identified through structural analysis of input/output relationships within each Bitcoin transaction.

Time and Value Analysis was a core technique applied throughout this case. Each transaction's UTC timestamp was recorded and correlated across networks to establish a precise chronological sequence of fund movements — from the initial theft on April 4, 2026 through to the final Bitcoin distributions on May 1, 2026. Simultaneously, the value of funds was tracked in both native token units and USD equivalent at each hop, allowing the analyst to confirm that the victim's 14.11 BNB (≈ $8,375) was consistently present within the larger consolidated pool of 40,551 USDT across the TRON and Bitcoin layers. This dual tracking of time and value confirmed that the funds did not exit to any exchange or third party and remain unspent at the time of this report.

Tools & Techniques Used
◈
Blockchain Explorers
BscScan (BNB Chain) · TronScan (TRON) · Bitaps (Bitcoin) · Bridgers.xyz (bridge) · NEAR Intents Explorer
◈
TXID Tracing
Each transaction was independently verified by its unique Transaction ID across the respective network explorer to confirm authenticity and direction of fund flow.
◈
Time & Value Analysis
UTC timestamps were correlated across all networks to establish a precise event sequence. Fund values were tracked in both native tokens and USD at every step to confirm continuity and rule out exits.
◈
Cross-Chain Bridge Analysis
Bridge-specific explorers were used to link the BSC deposit hash to the corresponding TRON destination hash, maintaining an unbroken trail across networks.
◈
Wallet Clustering
Addresses were grouped by behavioral patterns — shared inputs, sequential outputs, and timing — to attribute multiple wallets to the same controlling entity.
◈
Peeling Chain Analysis
The consistent output of exactly 0.16777215 BTC (2²⁴ − 1 satoshis) across four transactions was identified as a scripted peeling chain — a known Bitcoin laundering pattern for structured fund distribution.
◈
Token & USD Value Tracking
All amounts were recorded in both native cryptocurrency (BNB, USDT, BTC) and approximate USD equivalent to demonstrate the financial scale of the theft and confirm fund continuity.

Analysis & Findings
→Multi-chain fund movement across BNB Chain, TRON, and Bitcoin
→Use of cross-chain bridges to obscure fund traceability
→Conversion into USDT (TRC-20) prior to BTC conversion, adding an off-chain settlement layer
→Peeling chain pattern applied consistently (0.16777215 BTC per peel) — indicates scripted automation
→Subsequent partial consolidation of remaining change outputs into a single address
→No interaction with any identified exchange or cash-out service detected
Conclusion

The traced transactions demonstrate a deliberate and technically sophisticated obfuscation strategy, encompassing cross-chain transfers, stablecoin intermediation, and structured Bitcoin movements via a peeling chain. At the time of analysis, the funds remain under the control of the same threat entity and have not reached any identifiable exchange or cash-out point. Immediate asset freeze requests to relevant exchanges and continued on-chain monitoring are advised.

Risk Assessment
HIGH RISK
⚠ Unauthorized fund transfer confirmed
⚠ Multi-chain obfuscation strategy employed
⚠ Controlled and systematic fund distribution
⚠ No identifiable exit or cash-out point detected
Recommendation

Ongoing monitoring of all identified Bitcoin addresses is strongly recommended to detect future fund movements. Any interaction with a centralized exchange or custodial service will provide a potential point for further investigation. The residual dust output (bc1qngzxe4yxm73l8u5lev802a8e5xzgprzuh75dcp) should be specifically flagged, as small outputs are commonly used as tracking markers that signal the attacker's next transaction cluster.
